title: Product specifications are deterministic contract inputs
context: ADR-0002 established deterministic decisions, changes, evidence, and signed identity, but product user stories remained outside the compiled contract. A mutable story map could therefore change without changing the planHash, allowing implementation and review to observe different requirements while both appeared governed.
decision: OverlayKit retains the deterministic governance and signed-identity model established by ADR-0002 and adds typed product specifications as immutable, profile-selected contract inputs. The compiler validates specification actors, terms, requirements, user stories, Given/When/Then acceptance criteria, workflows, references, and supersession; it binds selected specification hashes and identifiers into the profileHash, GovernancePlan, and manifest. Version-two change contracts for architecture, product, security, or documentation work must reference at least one specification. Accepted specifications are changed only by an accepted successor.
consequences: The same decisions, specifications, profile, schemas, and mechanisms must compile byte-for-byte to the same planHash.
A product requirement is normative only when its accepted specification is selected by the active profile.
An accepted specification is immutable and may be replaced only through explicit supersession.
Version-two non-governance changes without a specification reference fail closed.
Evidence remains separate from law and cannot alter user stories or acceptance criteria.
Every pull-request commit and the exact workflow commit must remain GitHub-verified.
Independent approval remains deferred until a second trusted human can satisfy it without deterministic lockout.

policies.statement: Normative governance is the compiled plan derived from accepted immutable decisions, accepted immutable specifications, and the active profile.
A governance run may report outcomes only for gates and bindings already present in its matching plan and manifest.
An accepted decision is never edited or deleted; a successor decision derives supersession.
An accepted product specification is never edited or deleted; a successor specification derives supersession.
Version-two architecture, product, security, and documentation changes reference the specifications whose observable criteria authorize their scope.
Every change separates facts, inferences, assumptions, and unknowns; facts and inferences require evidence.
An agent identity has no independent project authority and must name its human principal.
A change is ready only when every enforced gate passed and every enforced artifact is present for the current planHash and manifestHash.

policies.description: The deterministic governance host validates and verifies the stored plan and manifest.
Typed change contracts satisfy their schema and epistemic invariants.
Lint, type-check, and automated tests pass.
A reviewer other than the last producer approves the change.
The workflow commit and every pull-request commit carry an identity signature verified by the GitHub root of trust.
Canonical plan.json and manifest.json generated from the contract.
A typed change contract containing scope, claims, criteria, and definition of done.
An independent review attached to the proposed change.
